goal: Doc convention: seal.json lives in .ml-cache/ to keep submit's worktree-clean check happy

summary.title: Doc convention: seal.json lives in .ml-cache/ to keep submit's worktree-clean check happy
summary.what: Move the documented seal-temp-file convention from `seal.json` (in the repo root) to `.ml-cache/seal.json`. Updates README quickstart and the agent guidance + IDE-stub templates; bumps templates to v12 / v8. Adds a one-paragraph explanation in the agent template covering *why* `.ml-cache/` (already gitignored by init, so the temp file stays out of git AND keeps the v0.3 worktree-clean check happy on submit).
summary.why: Smoke-tested the full trial-user loop end-to-end and caught a hard failure: a fresh user follows the README quickstart literally, runs `mainline seal --prepare > seal.json` in the repo root, then `mainline seal --submit < seal.json`, and gets `INVALID_STATUS: worktree is untracked; refusing to seal with incomplete evidence`. The temp file is the cause. Moving the convention into `.ml-cache/` (which init already writes to .gitignore) keeps the loop working without forcing trial users to learn the `--allow-dirty` flag.
summary.user_goal: A trial user follows the README quickstart literally and the seal flow succeeds without `--allow-dirty`.

decisions.point: where to put the temp file
decisions.rationale: Choices ranked: (a) .ml-cache/seal.json — already gitignored, no new behaviour, no Windows/macOS path differences; (b) /tmp/seal.json — needs cross-platform handling; (c) special-case 'seal.json' in the worktree check — too clever, brittle. (a) is the cheapest and most predictable.
The worktree-clean check is load-bearing for the v0.3 snapshot contract; weakening it to ignore one filename would invite worse drift. Telling users where to put the temp file is the right scope.

decisions.chose: One paragraph in the seal step explaining why `.ml-cache/`.
decisions.rationale: An agent reading the template on first contact and seeing an unusual path needs to know the choice was deliberate. Without the explanation, agents would 'helpfully' switch back to repo-root paths.

rejected.reason: Brittle: any user with their own seal.json file would have it silently ignored. The worktree contract should not depend on filenames.
Same brittleness as above. Some users legitimately have unrelated files named seal.json.

rejected.alternative: Recommend a shell pipeline (no temp file).
rejected.reason: Hard for trial users to type and read; the temp file is the conventional intermediate artifact for a multi-step JSON edit. Better to make the conventional path work.

risks: Users following old README snippets / blog posts that reference repo-root seal.json will still hit the original failure. Acceptable: the new docs are clear and the error message points at the worktree state.
Two consecutive template bumps (v11 → v12) on the trial-prep day means existing repos see two update_available cycles. Acceptable: agents update is idempotent and shows in `agents check`.

anti_patterns.what: Special-casing well-known filenames in the worktree-clean check
anti_patterns.why: The contract should depend on `git status`, not on a hardcoded filename allowlist. Users who legitimately track a file with that name would silently break the check.

anti_patterns.what: Adding `seal.json` to the gitignore init writes
anti_patterns.why: Init's gitignore is for Mainline's own state, not for naming conventions Mainline picks. A user with an unrelated seal.json file would have it silently ignored.

followups: Audit other docs (blog posts, FAQ, README's deeper sections) for `> seal.json` references.
Consider adding a `--seal-path` flag on prepare/submit so the convention is selectable for users who want a custom path.
Long-term: a `mainline seal --interactive` mode that handles the temp file internally and never exposes the path to the user.

architectural_claims: Documented temp-file conventions belong in already-gitignored locations so they don't trip the v0.3 worktree-clean snapshot contract.
The worktree-clean check should not special-case filenames; the convention does the work, not the validator.
behavioral_changes: README quickstart and agent guidance templates show `.ml-cache/seal.json` instead of `seal.json`.
Agent guidance template at v12; stub template at v8; existing repos see update_available on `mainline agents check`.

data_model_changes.name: agents-md and agents-stub template versions
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Bumped agents-md to v12, stub to v8. mainline agents update migrates in place; user content outside markers preserved.

migration_notes: Run `mainline agents update` to pick up the new convention.
